revision.comment: [[大野氏 (豊後国)]] へのリダイレクトを解除しました
肥後国、大野氏についての追記。
大野氏 (肥後国)のリンクを追加
正しい記事名に差し替え
石清水八幡宮リンク追加

revision.text:
'''大野氏'''（おおのし/おおのうじ）は日本の氏族のひとつ。下記以外にも多数の系統の氏族がある。
*[[大野氏 (武蔵国)]] - [[武蔵国]][[秩父郡]]大野村に起源を持つ。 *[[大野氏 (尾張国)]] - [[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]大野村に起源を持つ。清和源氏の宇野支流もしくは斯波支流。 *[[大野氏 (播磨国)]] - [[播磨国]]北条郷大野（[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]加古川町大野）に起源を持つ。[[宇多源氏]]を称する。[[文観|文観房弘真]]（[[後醍醐天皇]]に仕えた僧侶）を輩出。 *[[大野氏 (伊予国)]] - [[伊予国]][[喜多郡]]大野に起源を持つ。本姓は[[橘氏|橘]]姓とも藤姓とも、[[伊予親王]]の子孫ともいう。 *[[大野氏 (豊後国)]] - [[豊後国]][[大野郡]]大野郷に起源を持つ。[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神]]支流。 *[[大野氏 (肥後国)]] - 肥後国大野別府に起源を持つ (『関東下知状案』貞応3年5月21日)。京都[[石清水八幡宮]]、神主職 [[紀氏]]の支流。(小寺家本『石清水八幡宮略補任』)
* '''大野氏'''はまた[[鮮卑|鮮卑人]]の姓の一つ。[[李虎]]が[[姓]]を授けられた大野氏。
==関連項目== *[[大野城]] {{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおのし}}

revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] on (CatAlot)
revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}
{{Aimai}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Musashi Province|ja|秩父郡}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District, Owari Province|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Echizen Province|ja|大野郡 (福井県)}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|ja|加古川市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|ja|宇多源氏}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Iyo Province|ja|喜多郡}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana Clan|ja|橘氏}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Bungo Province|ja|大野郡}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|ja|鮮卑}} or {{ill|Gaoche|ja|高車}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu|ja|李虎}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|ja|大野城}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q61055684]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and bore the title [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, [[:en:Chichibu District, Saitama|Chichibu District, Musashi Province]], [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, [[:en:Chita District|Chita District, Owari Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]]. Descended from the Uno branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Clan|qq=|1=Asahi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517554}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from [[:en:Ōno District, Fukui|Ōno District, Echizen Province]], [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] (currently Ōno, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the [[:en:Uda Genji|Uda Genji]] and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|qq=|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in [[:en:Kita District, Ehime|Kita District, Iyo Province]], [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. Claimed lineages include the [[:en:Tachibana clan (kuge)|Tachibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District, Bungo Province]], [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōno Castle|Ōno Castle]]

Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and bore the title [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, [[:en:Chichibu District, Saitama|Chichibu District, Musashi Province]], [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, [[:en:Chita District|Chita District, Owari Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]]. Descended from the Uno branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Clan|qq=|1=Asahi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517554}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from [[:en:Ōno District, Fukui|Ōno District, Echizen Province]], [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] (currently Ōno, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the [[:en:Uda Genji|Uda Genji]] and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|qq=|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in [[:en:Kita District, Ehime|Kita District, Iyo Province]], [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. Claimed lineages include the [[:en:Tachibana clan (kuge)|Tachibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District, Bungo Province]], [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Claimed lineages include the [[:en:Tachibana clan (kuge)|Tachibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District, Bungo Province]], [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōno Castle|Ōno Castle]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q61055684]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and bore the title [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, [[:en:Chichibu District, Saitama|Chichibu District, Musashi Province]], [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, [[:en:Chita District|Chita District, Owari Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]]. Descended from the Uno branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Clan|1=Asahi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517554}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from [[:en:Ōno District, Fukui|Ōno District, Echizen Province]], [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] (currently Ōno, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the [[:en:Uda Genji|Uda Genji]] and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in [[:en:Kita District, Ehime|Kita District, Iyo Province]], [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. Claimed lineages include the [[:en:Tachibana clan (kuge)|Tachibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District, Bungo Province]], [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Claimed lineages include the [[:en:Tachibana clan (kuge)|Tachibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District, Bungo Province]], [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōno Castle|Ōno Castle]]

Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] (currently Ōno, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the [[:en:Uda Genji|Uda Genji]] and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in [[:en:Kita District, Ehime|Kita District, Iyo Province]], [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. Claimed lineages include the [[:en:Tachibana clan (kuge)|Tachibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District, Bungo Province]], [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōno Castle|Ōno Castle]]

Descended from the Uno branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Clan|1=Asahi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517554}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from [[:en:Ōno District, Fukui|Ōno District, Echizen Province]], [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] (currently Ōno, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the [[:en:Uda Genji|Uda Genji]] and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]]. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in [[:en:Kita District, Ehime|Kita District, Iyo Province]], [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]]. Claimed lineages include the [[:en:Tachibana clan (kuge)|Tachibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District, Bungo Province]], [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine]] in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the [[:en:Xianbei|Xianbei]] or [[:en:Kang-chü|Gaoche]] peoples. It was granted to [[:en:Li Hu (general)|Li Hu]] by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōno Castle|Ōno Castle]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q61055684]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|WD=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District, Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Owari Province}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Clan|1=Asahi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517554}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Fukui|en|Ōno District, Fukui|WD=Q8081656|lt=Ōno District, Echizen Province}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|en|Uda Genji|WD=Q4846201|lt=Uda Genji}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Go-Daigo}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Ehime|en|Kita District, Ehime|WD=Q1206404|lt=Kita District, Iyo Province}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana Clan}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District, Bungo Province}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|en|Xianbei|WD=Q861054|lt=Xianbei}} or {{ill|Kang-chü|en|Kang-chü|WD=Q1493790|lt=Gaoche}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu (general)|en|Li Hu (general)|WD=Q5370334|lt=Li Hu}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|en|Ōno Castle|WD=Q1206694|lt=Ōno Castle}}

Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|WD=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District, Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Owari Province}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Clan|1=Asahi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517554}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Fukui|en|Ōno District, Fukui|WD=Q8081656|lt=Ōno District, Echizen Province}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|en|Uda Genji|WD=Q4846201|lt=Uda Genji}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Go-Daigo}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Ehime|en|Kita District, Ehime|WD=Q1206404|lt=Kita District, Iyo Province}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana Clan}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District, Bungo Province}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|en|Xianbei|WD=Q861054|lt=Xianbei}} or {{ill|Kang-chü|en|Kang-chü|WD=Q1493790|lt=Gaoche}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu (general)|en|Li Hu (general)|WD=Q5370334|lt=Li Hu}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|WD=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District, Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Owari Province}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana Clan}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District, Bungo Province}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|en|Xianbei|WD=Q861054|lt=Xianbei}} or {{ill|Kang-chü|en|Kang-chü|WD=Q1493790|lt=Gaoche}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu (general)|en|Li Hu (general)|WD=Q5370334|lt=Li Hu}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏]]
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) refers to one of the Japanese clans. Apart from the lineage described below, many other clans share this name.
* The ancient aristocratic Ōno Clan was a branch of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and bore the title {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja|大野氏 (武蔵国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Musashi Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|WD=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District, Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja|大野氏 (尾張国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Owari Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Village, {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Owari Province}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}. Descended from the Uno branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} (also called the {{ill|Asahi Clan|ja|朝日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Clan|1=Asahi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517554}}). * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (越前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Echizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from {{ill|Ōno District, Fukui|en|Ōno District, Fukui|WD=Q8081656|lt=Ōno District, Echizen Province}}, {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}. Similarly descended from the Shiba branch of the Seiwa Genji. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja|大野氏 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Harima Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in Hokujō Township, {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}} (currently Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, Hyōgo Prefecture). Claimed descent from the {{ill|Uda Genji|en|Uda Genji|WD=Q4846201|lt=Uda Genji}} and produced figures such as {{ill|Bunkan|ja|文観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkan|1=Monkan-bō Kōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500110}}, a monk serving Emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Go-Daigo}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja|大野氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Iyo Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno in {{ill|Kita District, Ehime|en|Kita District, Ehime|WD=Q1206404|lt=Kita District, Iyo Province}}, {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}}. Claimed lineages include the {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana Clan}}, Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大野氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Township in {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District, Bungo Province}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. A branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Higo Province)|qq=}} - Originated from Ōno Beppu in {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}} (referenced in "Kantō Chijōjō-an," Jōō 3, 21st day of the 5th month). Connected to the {{ill|Kino Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kino Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of the priests of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū Shrine}} in Kyoto. * {{ill|Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja|大野氏 (肥前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Clan (Hizen Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Originated from Ōno Manor in {{ill|Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja|高木郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagi District, Hizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} (referenced in the "Fukae Family Documents," designated an Important Cultural Property of Saga Prefecture, March 16, 1987). * The '''Ōno Clan''' was also a surname of the {{ill|Xianbei|en|Xianbei|WD=Q861054|lt=Xianbei}} or {{ill|Kang-chü|en|Kang-chü|WD=Q1493790|lt=Gaoche}} peoples. It was granted to {{ill|Li Hu (general)|en|Li Hu (general)|WD=Q5370334|lt=Li Hu}} by Emperor {{ill|Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja|北魏の孝文帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaowen of Northern Wei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Ōno Castle|en|Ōno Castle|WD=Q1206694|lt=Ōno Castle}}
